Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The right frame is at rows 30-32, cols 30-32, but the target is at rows 26-30, cols 44-48. The frame didn't move as expected! It seems the UP and RIGHT actions didn't work, or the frame got blocked by the purple barrier.
Wait, the boundary shows [[30, 30], [30, 32], [32, 32], [32, 30]] which means rows 30-32, cols 30-32. Before the actions, it was at rows 30-32, cols 27-29. So it moved right by 3 cols (from 27-29 to 30-32) but didn't move up. The UP action was blocked, and only the first RIGHT worked (or maybe all 6 RIGHTs worked but the frame is now at cols 30-32 which is only 3 cols right from 27-29).
Wait, 27-29 to 30-32 is only one RIGHT move (3 cols). But I did 6 RIGHTs. So maybe the frame got blocked after one move by the purple barrier.
Let me check: the purple barrier is between the panels. The frame at cols 30-32 would be right at or past the barrier. Maybe it can only move within its panel.
So the right frame is stuck at cols 30-32. The right target is at cols 44-48, which is in the right panel. The frame needs to get to the right panel somehow.
Wait, but the frame is at cols 30-32 now, and the purple barrier is somewhere around cols 33-35. Maybe the frame can't pass through the barrier.
Hmm, but the target is in the right panel. How can the frame get there? Maybe I need to go around the barrier - up or down.
Let me try moving the frame UP first to go above the barrier, then RIGHT to cross to the right panel.
Actually wait - the UP action didn't work. Maybe the frame can't move up because it's blocked by something. Let me check the ascii to see the layout more clearly.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check ascii around the frame and barrier
ascii = current_frame.ascii
lines = ascii.split('\n')

# Print rows 25-35, cols 25-50 to see the layout
for r in range(25, 35):
    print(f"Row {r}: {lines[r][25:50]}")

# The frame is at rows 30-32, cols 30-32, target at rows 26-30, cols 44-48
# Frame didn't move UP - maybe blocked by something
# Try going UP first to clear the barrier, then RIGHT

# Let's try UP first to see if it works now
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
